opinion.h4: RUMMEL ET AL. v. MUSGRAVE ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF COLORADO. No. 303. Decided October 17, 1960.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Duke W. Dunbar, Attorney General of Colorado, Frank E. Hickey, Deputy Attorney General, and John W. Patterson, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
364 U.S. 293 PICCOTT v. FLORIDA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF FLORIDA. No. 112, Misc. Decided October 17, 1960.
Richard W. Ervin, Attorney General of Florida, and George R. Georgieff, Assistant Attorney General, for respondent.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
